transcript.text:
[40 Years of Tradition?]
[A perfect dish for cold weather]
[Fried crumbs?]
[Hello, I'm fried chicken crumbs]
[Fried crumb noodles]
[Tteokboki (spicy rice cake)]
[Kimchi Fried Rice]
[Jjolmyun (spicy chewy noodles)]
[Savory soup]
[Sujebi (hand-torn noodle soup)]
How can such a skinny person eat so much?
[It just]
[keeps]
[going in]
[Cleared]
Hello everyone

I'm here at Incheon to run some errands
There's this restaurant that I've been wanting to try
They only open for 4 hours on the weekdays
They have this noodle soup with fried crumbs on it
I've been wanting to try it out for a long time
[Incheon, Jung-gu] [Maet-dol Kalguksu]
There are a lot of menu items
[Beware: indecisive people]
So many that I want to eat
Excuse me,
What's jjol-udon?
It's chewy noodles with kalguksu soup
[A long time to make a decision]
Excuse me, Tteokboki,
Kalguksu,
Kaljebi,

Jjolmyun,
Jjol-udon,
[Let me get pen and papers]
[Tell me]
Kimchi fried rice as well
Why are you eating so much?
[That's just me]
[Let's get the noodles started]
[Pour the hot soup]
[Hand-torn noodles]
[With their special sauce]
[What is this?]
[Fried crumbs as topping]
[Fried kalguksu and fried kaljebi]
[Tteokboki too]
[Kimchi fried rice]

[Sesame oil]
[Cook it]
[Don't forget the fried eggs]
[Kimchi fried rice complete]
[There's still more]
[Jjolmyun]
[Complete]
This place has been here for 40 years
Kalguksu with 40 years of tradition
This area is known for their fried chicken
They use the crumbs as a topping
They give you a lot
Bon appetit
This is the highlight
That looks delicious
Crispy fried crumbs

It really is fried chicken
Hot hot
It's tasty
It's a little spicy
The fried crumbs play a part in giving soup extra flavor
Delicious
With some kimchi
I always choke on the heat when I eat hot noodles
Yummy
This is interesting
The soup is really refreshing
It has some spice to it
They're also known for their tteokboki
I can tell that this is flour-rice cake
Market tteokboki?
Familiar, yet tasty

It's yummy
This is spicy too Everything is spicy in general
Kaljebi this time
It's the same except they add hand-torn noodles in here
These are the tasty kind of ones
Hand-torn sujebi
You can just tell from its looks
I really love this one
Just like sujebi that grandmother used to make
Let's eat the fried rice now
It's unique
The rice is different
This is almost like a kimchi risotto
1 big spoonful
Yummy
This is just what I like

This is like a mixture of a risotto
It's good
Mix it together
Cut it
And put it on top
I don't really like sticky rice that much, but
It's got that consistency of a risotto
It's good
It's even better than the regular one
It's the best Delicious
Let's mix the Jjolmyun
It's still fresh
It looks really good
This usually comes with sliced cucumbers
I asked them not to put it in
This is really sweet and sour

This is the sauce that I really like
Let's hurry before it gets bloated
Jjol-udon this time
This is very similar to the mul-jjolmyun that I had at Okcheon
[1 year ago]
The noodles feel different from this one
This one has more texture
But this one is very soft
The noodles feel very different
It's a bit spicy
What? I'm already almost finished?
I'm eating too fast
They really do give you a lot though
Can you see the size?
I'll put this here
Market tteokboki

This is big too, right?
Delicious
Break this up
[She got quiet]
[Let's give her some time]
If I were a high school student here, I would become a regular here for the fried rice and the jjolmyun noodles
This is something that high school students would enjoy
I tend to find myself trying to balance what I eat
I eat this because this has that much
Since this one has this much, I eat that
You guys do that too, right?
Get the amount somewhat even
It's time for tteokboki now
It's spicy
It's really spicy
[I need a break]

Is it that spicy?
Is it the tteokboki?
No, that's not that spicy
Which is it then?
Are you not good with spicy food?
It's not spicy when I eat 1 serving, but it gets spicy when I eat 6 servings
[Then that's your fault]
You're eating it all though
I'm not done yet
Just taking a break because of the spiciness
Do you want some milk?
Really?
Thank you
It's not spicy... but it's spicy if you eat a lot
I'm good with spicy
I can do the spiciest noodle challenge if it's just 1 serving

Just 1 serving
It'll be spicy if you eat the whole plate
I'm sure people will struggle if it's a lot of spicy food
I'm not bad with spicy food, right?
You're fine
You're just in pain
It's not painful for 1 serving
Then just eat 1 serving
How can I survive by eating just 1 serving?
[Eating just 1 serving is more painful]
It's not this one
Kimchi fried rice is not spicy at all
Do you starve yourself before you do mukbang?
No, I ate a lot last night
30-40 servings of gopchang (beef small intestines)?
[Shock & Terror]

Is the fried chicken made from here? or...
It's the crumbs from the first batch the other restaurant makes
That's cool
I was wondering where they get this because chicken was not on the menu
They get it from the fried chicken shop next door
How can such a skinny person eat so much?
Well, I used to eat the whole bowl when I was skinny But now I can't even finish 1 bowl
That was great
Thanks everyone~ [Subtitles by Hyun Choi] [antharoni@gmail.com]
